UK University Financial Performance — Sustainable Growth

All 155 institutions from Tables 2–5, ranked into four sustainable-growth performance categories. Filter by category or turnover band, or isolate institutions in deficit or with limited liquidity. Twelve financial metrics each; income compares 2020 with 2025.
Performance category
Turnover band
Quick filters
Showing 0 of 155

Metric comparison across institutions

Pick a metric; bars sorted high → low, coloured by performance category.

Metric relationships

Scatter: choose axes. Point size ∝ 2025 income; colour = category.

Data table

Click any header to sort. Deficits flagged red on the left and in red text.
Cat 1 · strongest Cat 2 Cat 3 Cat 4 · weakest
Source. Tables 2–5, “Advancing Institutional Maturity (Part 2)”. Institutions are grouped into four sustainable-growth performance categories (Table 2 = first/strongest … Table 5 = fourth/weakest). Turnover bands are derived from 2025 income (>£1bn, £101m–£999m, <£100m) and reproduce the report’s stated counts exactly (10 / 102 / 43). RCI and MCR are ratios; growth, staff, cashflow and surplus are percentages; Income/FTE, Cashflow and Income are GBP; Liquidity is in days. Notes: City St George’s uses legacy City University figures; Royal Conservatoire of Scotland’s Income/FTE is N/A in the source; “Bishop Grosseteste University” appears as “Lincoln Bishop” in the source layout. Data embedded inline — works offline.
